item.title: For Science! Taste-testing the “Can of Soda + Boxed Cake Mix” Meme
item.description:
Ok, so a few weeks ago, @PeaceOnEarthGoodPornToAll posted this meme under the title "Soda Cake Thing" and proceeded to post videos where they tested out the bottom-left "Zesty Lemon Cake" one. They seemed to think it was pretty good, summarizing: "Verdict: it’s actually really tasty! Just don’t fuck the entire process up and I bet it’d be awesome." In the ensuing thread however, it was suggested that a PROPER test would involve making all the cakes to compare them. The person suggesting such then sanely demurred to do the same themselves, suggesting it might be too much of an investment. I, on the other hand, live by the mantra that "Anything worth doing is worth over-doing!" And so here we are. TL;DR There’s about to be a lot of questionable cake in mrputter’s house.
First thing first: I turned to my partner-in-crime (also known as: girlfriend) and confirmed she’d be ok with this ridiculous experiment. She’s not on Imgur, but I’ll refer to her as T-Rex as that’s similar to a username that she goes by elsewhere. I actually knew beforehand that I didn’t need to worry about T-Rex’ approval: if there’s anyone more on board with overboard endeavours like this (especially when food is involved) she’d be the one. A woman after my own heart. I mean: there’s a reason we live together. Second issue: because we live in Canada, not all the brands and variants mentioned in the original meme are (easily) available here, so we had to make some adjustments: • Betty Crocker Strawberry cake mix: not available. We just skipped this one. • Betty Crocker Hershey’s dark chocolate: not available. Substituted chocolate fudge. • Cherry Dr. Pepper: not available. Substituted regular Dr. Pepper. • Barq’s root beer: not (easily) available. Substituted A+W root beer. • Schweppes ginger ale: probably available, but not in that size, at the stores I went to. Substituted Canada Dry ginger ale. We ended up having to run around to about 7 or 8 different stores, but I eventually rounded everything up into the mise-en-place above. The Dollarama tinfoil pans add that extra bit of Ghetto Authenticity™.
I measured out 1 can’s worth (355mL) of each type of pop before adding it to the mix. Then stirred. And stirred. And stirred. Then into the tin pans! The cake mixes were very fragrant. Which I suppose is not that surprising. I considered getting a used hand-crank eggbeater from Goodwill but at the end of the day decided it wouldn’t add that much to the Bit. But 5 batches did leave my arm a bit tired. The Cherry one particularly was a pain to mix. In addition to the particles of cherry-flavoured things, the mix itself was rather lumpy, and it was a bit of work getting all the clumps of dry mix smoothed out. (Going back now, I just noticed that PeaceOnEarthGoodPornToAll used a stand mixer to do the mixing. I considered doing that too, but since I was making 5 batches, that seemed like it would be a(n even bigger) pain in the ass cleaning the mixer between every batch so I chose not to. In retrospect it might have been the better approach.)
5 cakes ready to go in the oven! It’s a lot of cake. But at least T-Rex will have plenty of cake to take to the office to have with lunch for the next... couple of weeks.
And finally all out of the oven. For consistency’s sake (Science!) I wanted to make sure they were baked in as uniform a manner as I could. So only ran them through the oven a few at a time. It took quite a while to get through them all. And then they needed to cool down (consistency again). But finally…
Time to eat! I had some frosting to go with it, but but ended up not adding it for the taste test. I wanted to evaluate the cakes just based on their essential cake-ness. T-Rex and I both scored them 5 points in each of 3 categories (taste, texture/mouthfeel, and overall impression). For a total of 30 points.
Perhaps now is the time to mention that I don’t actually have all that much of a sweet tooth, and cake isn’t particularly my favourite at the best of times? I’m actually not entirely certain whether--in a case like this--that’s a good thing or a bad thing. Does it help me be more objective? Or does it just mean my anti-cake bias will get in the way of things? I choose to believe the former, but *shrug*. In either case that would be offset by T-Rex who is an absolute cake fiend and will happily gobble down ALL the cake. At the end of the day, I handicapped myself, by basically bumping up all my scores by a couple of points - as consistently across the board as I could manage. That brought them roughly in line with T-Rex’ scores. Use that information as you read our evaluation however you wish.
The clear winner for both of us was the Lemon cake. (The same one, coincidentally, as the one that PeaceOnEarthGoodPornToAll chose to make. Interesting!) The Ginger Spice put in a strong showing with a surprisingly delightful gingery overtone. Unsure how much of this was due to the specific choice of Ginger Ale we used (Canada Dry). We were mixed on the chocolate ones, with each of us preferring a different one, although both agreed they were pretty crumbly in a “not great” way. And both of us found the Cherry unexpectedly disappointing (especially since both of us really like cherries in the general case). On the whole I found all the cakes to be a bit underwhelming. (But really: what was I expecting? See above re: not a big cake fan.) They’re pretty dense, without the eggs to help give them lift. They’re also really sticky/tacky and hard to get out of the pan without breaking up (even though I greased/floured the pan well). There was discussion in the previous post about using parchment paper on the bottom, which in any other case I would 100% do but I felt like baking spray was more in the “spirit” of this type of baking. That was a mistake. You absolutely need to use parchment paper. PeaceOnEarthGoodPornToAll experienced similar things WRT them sticking to the pan, but I thought that greasing the heck out of them might be sufficient. Nope. The flavours overall are actually quite decent, but that density gives them a very slightly “pasty” mouthfeel, which I don’t love. The first piece of each cake was ok, but I found I can’t eat very much before they start going downhill. And really, best bets are Lemon or Ginger Spice. I’d eat those again.
Frosting improves them a lot. XD
Cat tax: Espresso and Latte chilling on the cat tree. Watching. Judging. (Always judging.)
item.dc:creator: mrputter

item.title: After the devastating earthquake and tsunami hit Japan in 2011, chaos swept across the country. At the heart of it all was the Fukushima Daiichi nuclear power plant—damaged, unstable, and teetering on the edge of disaster. A monument to the workers
item.description: After the devastating earthquake and tsunami hit Japan in 2011, chaos swept across the country. At the heart of it all was the Fukushima Daiichi nuclear power plant—damaged, unstable, and teetering on the edge of disaster. As radiation levels soared and the cooling systems failed, the risk of a full nuclear meltdown loomed large. And then, something remarkable happened. A group of about 50 workers—engineers, technicians, and operators, many of them older, some even retired—chose to stay behind. They weren’t soldiers. They didn’t carry weapons. But they walked straight into danger, knowing exactly what was at stake. They worked in shifts, wearing heavy protective gear, often unsure if they’d make it out alive. Their mission was simple, but incredibly risky: to pump seawater into the overheating reactors and prevent a total meltdown. Machines couldn’t do it. Robots couldn’t either. It had to be them. They became known as the Fukushima 50. These men and women weren’t looking for medals or headlines. They just knew someone had to do it. If they failed, the consequences could be catastrophic—not just for Japan, but for the world. Their courage wasn’t loud. It wasn’t flashy. But it was real. They showed up when everything seemed lost. They risked their lives to buy time, to protect others, and to stop something unimaginable from happening. To this day, many of them have never spoken publicly. Some suffered long-term health effects. But what they did can’t be forgotten. In a world full of noise, the quiet bravery of the Fukushima 50 still echoes.
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: What Happened Today - #124
item.description: A democracy dies when systematic erasure makes entire communities disappear while everyone argues about social media posts. The government is stripping Harvey Milk's name from the ship commissioned in his honor, eliminating 90% of tribal college funding, and using collective punishment to deport entire families for crimes that their relatives committed. Our country is methodically disappearing the institutions and people that make America diverse, educated, and humane until only the approved remain visible. The void isn't an accident. It's the point. Meanwhile, don't rely on the algorithm. Let me send this free and private summary to you every night so that you never miss what's going on. Add your email on the homepage of GovBrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://go.govbrief.today/musk-disgusting-abomination 2. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-recission-9billion 3. https://go.govbrief.today/steel-tariffs-begin 4. https://go.govbrief.today/revoke-emergency-abortion 5. https://go.govbrief.today/prisons-hormone-therapy 6. https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-renames-harvey-milk 7. https://go.govbrief.today/hegseth-skips-nato 8. https://go.govbrief.today/baraka-sues-habba 9. https://go.govbrief.today/biden-pardons-investigated 10. https://go.govbrief.today/democratic-fec-challenge 11. https://go.govbrief.today/doj-drops-navarro-suit 12. https://go.govbrief.today/fbi-raid-nazi 13. https://go.govbrief.today/chemical-safety-board 14. https://go.govbrief.today/humanitarian-parole-girl 15. https://go.govbrief.today/soliman-family-custody 16. https://go.govbrief.today/tribal-colleges-defunded 17. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-social-media-volume Yesterday's Story - https://go.govbrief.today/nadler-aide-investigation Fighting Back - https://go.govbrief.today/resource-disappearing-people Get Free In Email Daily - https://govbrief.today

item.title: The 513th vol. of "I'm not actually removing these from my phone, I just wanted to share them so I could feel some sort of connection in this dismal world" Dumps - Pride Month 2025 ep 3
item.description:
I just realized/was informed in 2023 that I am actually bisexual. (I had just turned 39) Apparently, guys that are actually straight don't have a list of "just a couple of dudes they'd definitely fuck"
I am here for all of you. I will fight for your rights, because they are OUR rights. Everyone's. I will support you all no matter the consequences to myself.
It does, tho
To get who? That TERF, Rowling?
lesbiabs
STAY TUNED! I'll be releasing more MeeMays back into the wild *every other* Monday-Friday around 11:30 am, GMT-5, in my ""I'm not actually removing these from my phone, I just wanted to share them so that I could feel some sort of connection in this dismal world" Dumps" Or use Imgur's "Follow" Feature, which will alert you either when I post or when I go Viral. If you wish to chat, I'm frequently in ElbowDeepInAHorse and PotatoCheesMonk's Discord Servers between the hours of 7pm and 1am GMT-5 during the week, and at unpredictable but frequent times Saturdays and Sundays. These are both VERY safe places for LGBTQIA+ people, if you need a community, please stop in! AbbiiStabbii's server is also amazing, *especially* safe for Trans folk.
Sounds gay, I'm in
No. No they will not. This isn't fat-shaming, btw, it's gluttony-shaming. Some bodies get big no matter what you do. Others get big because you do everything to excess.
ACAB
Unfortunately, it's going to happen again unless we stop them
My sister is a married lesbian and did not have an epic wedding like this.
I'm at least 2 of these
That's actually pretty fuckin rad, tho
It's actually the beginning of June, but whatever
Ope. How'd this get in here? Are bugs gay?
31 now
HELL YEAH!
If necessary, absolutely
This is my newly commissioned Signature Image, by Dark Arts Studios. He's no longer on The Imgurs, I met him on ElbowDeepInAHorse's Discord Server. Here's where you can see his work: https://twitter.com/Anubis23342060 https://www.furaffinity.net/user/thed4rk1ord/ https://tailsofthegods.com/ https://dark-arts.itch.io/ FUN TIP / LIFE FACT / PRO HACK I upvote 90% - 99% of positive or neutral comments on my posts, as well as ≈50% of negative ones Archive Of Previous Dumps: Vols 1-300: https://imgur.com/a/vAfF9Wz Vols 301+ : https://imgur.com/a/oDakPaq Updated Whenever I Can Get Around To It #LGBTQIA #PrideMonth #Pride_Month #Pride #LGBTPride #Phone_Dump #Meme_Dump #Memes #MeeMays #AdolfHitlerSchoolForTheArts #TheWhoring20s #SaveTheBEEEEEES #BeeLM #SaveTheBees #JettfreyEpsfuelCandnt #CommeltSteelBeamicide #WhatIfNobodyShotJFK #AndHisHeadJustDidThat #EatingAppsTakingNaps #EatNamesAndTakeAss #MasturbatingIsNecrophilia #IfYoureDeadInside #DumpyMcGeeAndGuyNamedSteve #2Dumps1Site #AllTiddiesAreGoodTiddies #FreeTheNipple #GregFromHR #SteveFromAccounting #DougFromSales

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1371 (soup fantasies)
item.description:
Let's get this bread.
I broke my bedside table last night, which was exciting.
Phone interview with Superstition Meadery today
And I finally got a call about my car. Damage is going to be $800. Rotors and brakes all around are shot. Current plan is to take 400 from the bank, put 400 on the emergency credit card, and pray like hell a job is forthcoming in the next two weeks.
This sounds like a terrible cheese.
Big mind wanderer here.
Help.
I disagree but it's not the worst.
It me.
Song Sparrow (not my photograph)
Unfortunately, sometimes, it actively intervenes in my to-do list.
Yuuuuuuup
Huitzilopochtli wins.
Arthropleura (probably misspelled) was the biggest arthropod that ever lived, and if I remember correctly, it was as long as a shorter bus.
Oh, but that's not profitable for our shareholders innit?
A beautiful ending.
Cortez and Malinali.
Isn't there an isotope of iron that's radioactive? What would the Fae do with that?
Varangian guard
Religious syncretism happens even when you're not paying attention.
I think this is Early Cretaceous Maryland.
Baby rex
So tired of this
Pippin tax. Today's message from Pippin: "okay. Fuck you. Good boy. I love you too." ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! If you're a woman or minority in a STEM field in Arizona living north of Phoenix and you need new headshots for this website or another detailing your experiences/research/accomplishment because our monstrous regime has deleted your experience from an official government website, PM me. Pippin and I have a good Nikon DSLR, and we'll do the photos for free, and, uh, you'll get to meet Pippin, I guess? (If you know someone who could use this who's not on Imgur, feel free to PM me to get my email address for them and tell them they're free to message me.) 3) Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 4) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 5) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride

item.title: How much is too much
item.description: An Ohio man took his elderly dog Tinkerbell to a veterinarian clinic to be euthanized. Upon being told the fee for such a service is 5 hundred dollars, decided that's too much and strangled the dog to death in the vet clinic. The last moments of this poor dog's life were spent in fear and betrayal because the owner wanted to save a few bucks. 5 hundred dollars does seem high, but come on, there's got to be a better option. I'm confident a local humane society would have a lower cost, or even a no cost alternative if you're hard up. https://www.whio.com/news/local/man-refuses-pay-500-veterinarian-fee-strangles-his-dog/TPO7EIK5GBC4NBIZBV3AJWY5GY/
item.dc:creator: SixtyPercentOfTheThymeItWorksEveryThyme

item.title: Never forget the mass murder of Chinese seeking rights, freedoms, democracy.
item.description: The same people who carried out these murders still hold power in China. Rights continue to be arbitrarily violated. Summary death sentences are common. Millions of people are jailed without cause. Some day there will be a monument to the thousands killed on June 4th. https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-china-42465516
